What is done in acts of devotion and charity should be done willingly, not by force; for God loves a cheerful giver. David set a good example. This offering from David was not given out of obligation or to show off; but because he had devoted himself to the house of God, and thought he could never do enough to support that worthwhile project. Those who want to lead others to goodness must go first.

We cannot form a correct idea of the magnificence of the temple and the buildings around it, concerning which so much gold and silver were used. But the boundless riches of Christ exceed the splendor of the temple, infinitely more than that surpassed the simplest home on earth. Instead of boasting about these large gifts, David gave sincere thanks to the Lord. Everything they gave for the Lord’s temple belonged to Him; if they tried to keep it, death would soon remove them from it. The only way they could benefit from it was to dedicate it to the service of Him who gave it.

This great assembly joined with David in worshiping God. Whoever is speaking on behalf of the group benefits only when they join him, not by bowing their head so much as by lifting up their spirit. Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord. Solomon’s kingdom represented the kingdom of the Messiah, whose throne is the throne of the Lord.

When we read the second book of Samuel, we could hardly have expected to see David appear so distinguished in his final moments. But his repentance had been as remarkable as his sin; and his behavior during his suffering and toward the end of his life seemed to have a good effect on his people. Blessed be God, even the greatest sinners can hope for a glorious ending when they turn from their sins and flee for refuge to the Savior’s atoning blood. Let us observe the difference between the spirit and character of the man after God's own heart, living and dying, and those of worthless pretenders, who resemble him in nothing but their sins, and who wickedly try to excuse their crimes by his sins. Let us watch and pray so that we are not overcome by temptation and caught up in sin, to the dishonor of God and the wounding of our own consciences. When we feel that we have offended, let us follow David's example of repentance and patience, looking for a glorious resurrection through our Lord Jesus Christ.
